title: Frontend: ATSAnalysis component + TypeScript types update
description:
Add ATS scoring UI to the frontend. This depends on task-15 (backend) which defines the data shape. ## Files to create / modify ### 1. `frontend/src/types/api.ts` — ADD new types Append after the `CVContentAnalysis` interface (keep the file structure consistent): ```typescript export type ATSKeywordImportance = "critical" | "important" | "nice_to_have"; export interface ATSKeyword { keyword: string; found_in_cv: boolean; importance: ATSKeywordImportance; } export type ATSCandidateMatch = "full" | "partial" | "none"; export interface ATSRequirementMatch { requirement: string; candidate_match: ATSCandidateMatch; /** Text from CV that supports this match; empty string if none */ evidence: string; /** What is missing to achieve a full match; empty string if fully met */ gap: string; } export interface ATSPriorityGap { /** 1 = critical deal-breaker, 2 = important, 3 = nice-to-have */ priority: 1 | 2 | 3; description: string; } export interface ATSAnalysis { ats_score: number; // 0–100 keyword_matches: ATSKeyword[]; requirement_matches: ATSRequirementMatch[]; priority_gaps: ATSPriorityGap[]; tailoring_suggestions: string[]; } ``` Also update `AnalysisResult` to add: ```typescript ats_analysis: ATSAnalysis | null; // null when no JD provided or task failed ``` Also add `"ATS_SCORING"` to the `AnalysisStatus` union type. ### 2. `frontend/src/components/ATSAnalysis.tsx` — NEW FILE Create a new component following the exact patterns of `ContentAnalysis.tsx` and `ScoreGauge.tsx`. **Props interface:** ```typescript interface ATSAnalysisProps { atsAnalysis: ATSAnalysis | null; jobDescriptionProvided: boolean; } ``` **Empty state** (no job description provided): - Render the card with title "ATS Match Score". - Show a muted info box: "Provide a job description to get ATS scoring." - Use a style consistent with the info/empty states in other components (e.g. a light gray or blue-50 rounded box with small text). **Null state** (job description was provided but task failed — `atsAnalysis === null && jobDescriptionProvided === true`): - Show the card with title "ATS Match Score". - Show an amber warning box: "ATS scoring unavailable for this result." **Populated state** (`atsAnalysis !== null`): **ATS Score gauge** — render a smaller version of the radial gauge from `ScoreGauge.tsx`: - Reuse the `RadialBarChart / RadialBar / PolarAngleAxis` pattern from recharts (already a dependency). - Use width=140, height=90, innerRadius=55, outerRadius=78, barSize=13 (smaller than seniority gauge). - Color thresholds: score ≤ 40 → red, ≤ 70 → amber, > 70 → green (same hex values as ScoreGauge). - Below the gauge show the numeric score and a label: "Low ATS Match" / "Moderate ATS Match" / "Strong ATS Match". **Keyword Match Table:** - Section heading: "Keyword Coverage". - Table with columns: Keyword | Importance | Found. - `importance` badges: critical → `bg-red-100 text-red-700`, important → `bg-amber-100 text-amber-700`, nice_to_have → `bg-gray-100 text-gray-600`. - `found_in_cv` indicator: true → green checkmark SVG icon, false → red X SVG icon (inline, 16x16, heroicons style, `stroke-current`). - Sort rows: missing critical first, then missing important, then missing nice-to-have, then found keywords last. - If `keyword_matches` is empty, show nothing for this section. **Requirements Mapping:** - Section heading: "Requirements Mapping". - For each `ATSRequirementMatch` render a card row: - `candidate_match` badge: full → `bg-green-100 text-green-700 "Full"`, partial → `bg-amber-100 text-amber-700 "Partial"`, none → `bg-red-100 text-red-700 "None"`. - Requirement text in medium weight. - If `evidence` is non-empty: show a small "Evidence" label + the evidence text in a `bg-green-50` box. - If `gap` is non-empty: show a small "Gap" label + the gap text in a `bg-red-50` box. - Divide rows with `divide-y divide-gray-100`. - If `requirement_matches` is empty, show nothing for this section. **Priority Gaps:** - Section heading: "Priority Gaps". - Group by priority. Render three sub-sections only if items exist for that priority: - Priority 1: heading "Critical — Deal-breakers" with `text-red-600`. - Priority 2: heading "Important — Qualifications Missing" with `text-amber-600`. - Priority 3: heading "Nice-to-have Gaps" with `text-gray-500`. - Each gap is a bullet point list item (same `w-1 h-1 rounded-full` dot pattern as ScoreGauge). - If `priority_gaps` is empty, show nothing for this section. **Tailoring Suggestions:** - Section heading: "Tailoring Suggestions". - Numbered list of `tailoring_suggestions` strings. - Use `bg-blue-50 rounded-md px-3 py-2 text-sm` per item, with a leading index number. - If `tailoring_suggestions` is empty, show nothing for this section. **Overall card structure:** ```tsx

ATS Match Score

{/* ...sections... */}
``` ### 3. `frontend/src/pages/AnalyzerPage.tsx` — ADD component to results The component is already imported from nearby files using named imports. Add: ```typescript import { ATSAnalysis } from '../components/ATSAnalysis'; ``` In the results section, after `` add: ```tsx ``` Derive `jobDescriptionProvided` as: `statusQuery.data.result.score_breakdown.job_fit_adjusted || statusQuery.data.result.ats_analysis !== null`. This covers both the case where ATS succeeded and where it failed but a JD was provided (job_fit_adjusted would be true if compute_score used the JD). Actually, the cleanest signal available from the result is `score_breakdown.job_fit_adjusted` — use that as the `jobDescriptionProvided` prop. ## Acceptance Criteria - `ATSAnalysis | null` field is present on `AnalysisResult` TypeScript type. - `"ATS_SCORING"` is present in `AnalysisStatus` union. - When `atsAnalysis === null && !jobDescriptionProvided`: card shows empty-state message. - When `atsAnalysis === null && jobDescriptionProvided`: card shows degraded/warning message. - When `atsAnalysis` is populated: gauge, keyword table, requirements, priority gaps, and suggestions all render. - No TypeScript compile errors (`cd frontend && pnpm tsc --noEmit`). - Component follows the same Tailwind class patterns as existing components (no new dependencies needed — recharts is already installed). - `ATSAnalysis` component is rendered in `AnalyzerPage` results section after `ContentAnalysis`.
